title: Testosterone result review

examination.text: Supports documenting compatible symptoms, consistently low morning testosterone context, reliable assay or reference-range context and clinician-selected LH, FSH or further evaluation.
examination.rationale: The exact diagnostic section supports result source and date, compatible symptom and negative context, focused examination and clinician-reviewed hormone results without diagnosing hypogonadism.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting compatible symptoms, consistently low morning testosterone context, reliable assay or reference-range context and clinician-selected LH, FSH or further evaluation.

source.title: Testosterone Therapy in Men With Hypogonadism: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.4 — symptoms, repeat morning result and cause evaluation

investigations.text: Supports documenting fertility plans, comorbidity and contraindication context and shared benefit-risk discussion; no therapy is selected.
investigations.rationale: The exact individualized risk-discussion section supports patient goals, measured weight, clinician plan and questions when documented; it does not select testosterone therapy.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting fertility plans, comorbidity and contraindication context and shared benefit-risk discussion; no therapy is selected.

source.title: Testosterone Therapy in Men With Hypogonadism: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 2.1–2.6 — individualized treatment and risk discussion

referral.text: Supports clinician review of response, adverse effects, adherence and monitoring after treatment initiation without generating thresholds, doses or referral.
referral.rationale: The exact monitoring section supports prior specialist correspondence, safety-net documentation and arranged follow-up only when treatment or review is clinician-led.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician review of response, adverse effects, adherence and monitoring after treatment initiation without generating thresholds, doses or referral.

source.title: Testosterone Therapy in Men With Hypogonadism: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 3.1–3.2 — response, adverse effects and monitoring

sources_used.title: Testosterone Therapy in Men With Hypogonadism: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
sources_used.jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing and referral policy governs local use

source_limitations: Sex, age, symptoms, sampling time, fasting, repeat value, assay, reference range, LH/FSH, fertility, comorbidity, medicines, interpretation and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
